question:
We have established a scenario in which four patients are treated with an intervention that is successful 75% of the time. The number of patients who respond to treatment is a binomial random variable with parameters n = 4 and p = 0.75. It follows that probability of a failureg = 1 - p=1 - 0.75= 0.25: (a)What is the probability of observing no successes among the four treatments?
(b)What is the probability of one success?
answer:
$$
 egin{aligned}
& 	ext { (a) } \operatorname{Pr}(X=0)={ }_n C_x \cdot p^x \cdot q^{n-x}={ }_4 C_0 \cdot 0.75^0 \cdot 0.25^{4-0}=1 \cdot 1 \cdot \
& 0.0039=0.0039
\end{aligned}
$$


(b)

$$
 egin{aligned}
& \operatorname{Pr}(X=1)={ }_n C_x \cdot p^x \cdot q^{n-x}={ }_4 C_1 \cdot 0.75^1 \cdot 0.25^{4-1}=4 \cdot 0.75 \cdot \
& 25=0.0469 .
\end{aligned}
$$


The probability of two successes is

$$
\operatorname{Pr}(X=2)={ }_4 C_2 \cdot 0.75^2 \cdot 0.25^{4-2}=6 \cdot 0.5625 \cdot 0.0625=0.2109
$$


The probability of three successes is

$$
\operatorname{Pr}(X=3)={ }_4 C_3 \cdot 0.75^3 \cdot .25^{4-3}=4 \cdot 0.4219 \cdot 0.25=0.4219
$$


The probability of four successes is

$$
\operatorname{Pr}(X=4)={ }_4 C_4 \cdot 0.75^4 \cdot 0.25^{4-4}=1 \cdot 0.3164 \cdot 1=0.3164
$$


Table 6.1 lists this pmf in tabular form.

 egin{tabular}{lccccc}
\hline \multicolumn{6}{l}{ Table 6.1. Probability mass function for $X \sim b(4,0.75)$ in tabular form. } \
\hline No. of successes $(x)$ & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 \
$\operatorname{Pr}(X=x)$ & 0.0039 & 0.0469 & 0.2109 & 0.4219 & 0.3164 \
\hline
\end{tabular}
